title: Quality control and planning engineers
description: Apply knowledge of engineering, biology, chemistry, computer science, and biomechanical principles to the design, development, and evaluation of biological, agricultural, and health systems and products, such as artificial organs, prostheses, instrumentation, medical information systems, and health management and care delivery systems. Perform engineering duties in planning, designing, and overseeing construction and maintenance of building structures and facilities, such as roads, railroads, airports, bridges, harbors, channels, dams, irrigation projects, pipelines, power plants, and water and sewage systems. Develop plans for surface transportation projects, according to established engineering standards and state or federal construction policy. Prepare designs, specifications, or estimates for transportation facilities. Plan modifications of existing streets, highways, or freeways to improve traffic flow. (Based on 42 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Simulate reservoir performance for different recovery techniques, using computer models.

Design, implement, and monitor the development of mining facilities, systems, or equipment, and assess operational effectiveness by monitoring production rates and analyzing data to recommend improvements.
Prepare technical reports for use by engineering and management personnel and document findings in detail to support decision-making and production improvements.
Evaluate production costs, capabilities, and economic value, and provide recommendations for optimizing oil and gas recovery and project feasibility based on safety, cost, and efficiency.
Inspect production sites to ensure safety compliance and evaluate soil, water, and air pollution management practices, as well as the effectiveness of mine safety programs and equipment.
Select or design equipment or procedures to improve production efficiency, quality control, and resource utilization while adhering to environmental regulations and standards.

Conduct research to investigate and validate new methodologies, technologies, and processes, including statistical analyses and modeling to enhance operational performance across mining and related engineering fields.
Develop and implement training programs for staff to improve operational skills, ergonomics, and safety practices within mining and production environments.
Evaluate and design layouts for efficient workspace and equipment use to optimize production workflows and minimize waste.
Analyze traffic systems or transportation scenarios to determine the effectiveness of existing infrastructure and recommend modifications or expansions.
Develop methodologies and protocols for validation and operational compliance testing regarding manufacturing processes or environmental standards, ensuring adherence to regulatory requirements.
Coordinate and oversee quality control activities and implement corrective actions for production issues based on validated standards and operational criteria.
Innovate and apply testing methodologies to assess operational issues, product designs, and material performance, ensuring compliance with industry standards.
Assist in scheduling and managing production workflows according to established timelines, material needs, and resource availability to facilitate efficient operations.
Communicate and collaborate with multidisciplinary teams to integrate user needs, regulatory considerations, and technical specifications into effectively designed systems and products.
Perform and document feasibility studies for projects and assess their potential impacts on environmental, economic, and community outcomes.
Direct and oversee research and development efforts for novel mining solutions, including assessments of new equipment needs and resource extraction methods.
Conduct audits and assessments of existing processes, equipment, and regulatory compliance to promote continual improvement and risk mitigation strategies.
Investigate operational challenges and develop recommendations for resolving issues related to production efficiency, resource utilization, and safety standards.
Analyze and resolve human factors related to ergonomics and usability of production processes or machinery to enhance worker safety and efficiency.
Facilitate the design and operation of decentralized water and wastewater treatment systems to comply with environmental regulations and efficiency standards.
Conduct environmental assessments and research to inform the design and implementation of water resource management programs and treatment technologies.
Develop and implement quality assurance and compliance documentation to support the validation of engineering and production processes within mining and water treatment systems.
Engage in continuous professional development to remain current with technological advancements and industry best practices in engineering and production.
Develop or assist in the development of transportation-related computer software or computer processes.
Prepare project budgets, schedules, or specifications for labor or materials.
Prepare final project layout drawings that include details such as stress calculations.
Participate in contract bidding, negotiation, or administration.
Investigate traffic problems and recommend methods to improve traffic flow or safety.
Design or prepare plans for new transportation systems or parts of systems, such as airports, commuter trains, highways, streets, bridges, drainage structures, or roadway lighting.
Investigate or test specific construction project materials to determine compliance with specifications or standards.
Inspect completed transportation projects to ensure safety or compliance with applicable standards or regulations.
Direct the surveying, staking, or laying-out of construction projects.
Estimate transportation project costs.
Confer with contractors, utility companies, or government agencies to discuss plans, specifications, or work schedules.
Check construction plans, design calculations, or cost estimations to ensure completeness, accuracy, or conformity to engineering standards or practices.
Analyze environmental impact statements for transportation projects.
Supervise the maintenance or repair of transportation systems or system components.
Gather and analyze water use data to forecast water demand.
Identify environmental risks and develop risk management strategies for civil engineering projects.
Conduct environmental impact studies related to water and wastewater collection, treatment, or distribution.
Manage and direct the construction, operations, or maintenance activities at project site.
Design energy-efficient or environmentally sound civil structures.
Design or engineer drainage, erosion, or sedimentation control systems for transportation projects.
Design transportation systems or structures with sustainable materials or products, such as porous pavement or bioretention structures.
Evaluate construction project materials for compliance with environmental standards.
Perform experiments and computer modeling to study the nature, structure, and physical and chemical properties of materials and their responses to applied forces.
Prepare reports, manuscripts, proposals, and technical manuals for use by other scientists and requestors.
Supervise and monitor production processes to ensure efficient use of equipment and project completion within time frame and budget.
Provide technical advice to industrial or managerial personnel regarding design, construction, program modifications, or structural repairs.
Plan laboratory experiments to confirm feasibility of processes and techniques used in the production of materials with special characteristics.
Conduct cost-benefit analyses for the construction of water supply systems, runoff collection networks, or wastewater systems.
Analyze survey reports, maps, drawings, blueprints, or other topographical or geologic data.
Evaluate the operation and maintenance of water or wastewater systems to identify ways to improve their efficiency.
Design and create plans for domestic or industrial water or wastewater treatment plants, including advanced facilities.
Design pumping systems, pumping stations, pipelines, or sewers for the collection of wastewater.
Design water distribution systems for potable or non-potable water.
Analyze and recommend chemical, biological, or other wastewater treatment methods to prepare water for use.
Analyze storm water or floodplain drainage systems to control erosion or stabilize river banks.
Prepare or present public reports on topics such as bid proposals or environmental impact statements.
Train new technicians or other personnel on relevant techniques in the field.
Maintain project logbook records or computer program files.
Compile and evaluate statistical data to determine and maintain quality and reliability of products.
Analyze data related to traffic flow, accident rates, or proposed development to determine the most efficient methods to expedite traffic flow.
Monitor street or utility projects for compliance to traffic control permit conditions and evaluate traffic control measures for permit approvals.
Collect or compile nanotechnology research or engineering data and gather verification data using equipment such as GPS systems or cameras.
Identify quality problems related to traffic systems and recommend solutions or improvements accordingly.
Prepare documentation or presentations, including charts, photos, or graphs based on testing procedures and results.
Inspect or measure various materials and components, including traffic control devices and emission outputs.
Collaborate with engineering teams to design or develop solutions for quality control and operational efficiency in traffic systems and other projects.
Oversee support staff and direct operations for project management effectiveness.
Maintain accurate records or logbooks for lab operations and testing procedures to ensure compliance and traceability.
Participate in out-of-specification and failure investigations and recommend corrective actions.
Conduct functional tests or assessments of systems, materials, or components to ensure operational efficiency and safety standards are met.
Follow regulations and safety procedures pertaining to quality control, hazardous materials, and other compliance requirements.
Train other staff or analysts to perform laboratory procedures and assays, ensuring knowledge transfer and operational consistency.
Assist in developing plans or modifications for construction projects, including obtaining permits for traffic and construction-related activities.
Evaluate and recommend improvements to production or testing processes based on performance data and compliance requirements.
Inspect facilities and equipment to ensure adherence to safety and quality control standards.
Ensure lab cleanliness and safety standards are maintained and oversee cleanup procedures related to hazardous materials.
Collaborate with technical staff from related fields to assess and improve quality and safety in bioproduction processes.
Modify existing processes or experimental techniques to enhance the functionality and compliance of equipment used in various projects.
Evaluate robotic systems or prototypes, build, configure, or test robots or robotic applications, and provide technical support for robotic systems.

Communicate operating characteristics or performance experience to other engineers or designers for training or new product development purposes, and document robotic application development, maintenance, or changes.

Create or maintain formal engineering documents, such as schematics, bills of materials, components or materials specifications, or packaging requirements, and prepare technical reports, data summary documents, or research articles for scientific publication, regulatory submissions, or patent applications.
Design or develop medical diagnostic or clinical instrumentation, equipment, or procedures, and evaluate product designs for safety.
Conduct or coordinate worker training in areas such as safety laws and regulations, hazardous condition monitoring, and use of safety equipment, and write and revise safety regulations and codes.

Prepare project plans for equipment or facility improvements, including timelines, budgetary estimates, or capital spending requests; manage teams of engineers by creating schedules, tracking inventory, creating or using budgets, or overseeing contract obligations or deadlines; and maintain technical project files.

Investigate industrial accidents, injuries, or occupational diseases to determine causes and preventive measures, and report or review findings from accident investigations, facilities inspections, or environmental testing.

Develop or file intellectual property and patent disclosure or application documents related to microelectromechanical systems (MEMS) devices, products, or systems; propose product designs involving MEMS technology, considering market data or customer requirements; and develop statistical models or simulations, using statistical or modeling software.

Conduct acceptance tests, vendor-qualification protocols, surveys, audits, corrective-action reviews, or performance monitoring of incoming materials or components to ensure conformance to specifications; evaluate materials, fabrication methods, joining methods, surface treatments, or packaging to ensure acceptable processing, performance, cost, sustainability, or availability.

Prepare energy-related project reports or related documentation, review architectural, mechanical, or electrical plans or specifications to evaluate energy efficiency, and verify energy bills and meter readings.

Advise clients or colleagues on topics such as climate control systems, energy modeling, data logging, sustainable design, or energy auditing; and recommend procedures for detection, prevention, and elimination of physical, chemical, or other product hazards.
Conduct research to evaluate safety levels for products, and adapt or design computer hardware or software for medical science uses.

Conduct tests of nuclear fuel behavior and cycles or performance of nuclear machinery and equipment to optimize performance of existing plants, and oversee the work of contractors in accordance with project requirements.
Integrate robotics with peripherals, such as welders, controllers, or other equipment, and create mechanical design documents for parts, assemblies, or finished products.

Conduct experimental or virtual studies to investigate characteristics and processing principles of potential microelectromechanical systems (MEMS) technology, and research new materials to be used for products, such as implanted artificial organs.
Conduct or direct testing of air quality, noise, temperature, or radiation levels to verify compliance with health and safety regulations.
Monitor nuclear facility operations to identify any design, construction, or operation practices that violate safety regulations and laws or could jeopardize safe operations.
Evaluate the safety, efficiency, and effectiveness of biomedical equipment, and examine accidents to obtain data for use in design of preventive measures.
Develop new approaches to improve existing designs or processes, and provide expert testimony in litigation cases.
Identify materials appropriate for mechatronic system designs and direct the purchase, modification, installation, testing, maintenance, and operation of fire prevention and protection systems.
Conduct harsh environmental testing, accelerated aging, device characterization, or field trials to validate devices.
Monitor energy-related design or construction issues, such as energy engineering, energy management, or sustainable design.
Plan mobile robot paths and teach path plans to robots.

Analyze system performance or operational requirements, design photonics systems or components, conduct testing to determine functionality or establish limits, oversee or provide expertise on manufacturing processes, and monitor and analyze energy consumption to ensure quality and efficiency in engineering projects.

Conduct research on new photonics technologies, investigate mechanical failures or unexpected maintenance problems, conduct engineering site audits, and develop or test photonic prototypes or models to improve design and implementation processes.
Design or coordinate the design of solar energy systems, create plans for system development, and develop specifications and functional requirements for renewable energy systems and components.

Prepare documents describing protocols and standards for equipment use, conduct training for personnel on energy management and proper use of equipment, and provide technical guidance on nanosystem start-up or maintenance.
Monitor energy systems for potential savings, perform thermal or cost reduction analyses, and recommend changes to improve system efficiency and compliance with specifications.
Train operators or other personnel, supervise technologists or technicians in research or production, and conduct training sessions on new material products and applications.

Conduct quality control tests on raw materials or products to ensure their quality, analyze product failure data, and review new product plans for material selection based on properties such as strength and cost.
Provide engineering technical support to designers on topics like wind turbines, oversee construction compliance with regulations, and conduct environmental studies related to energy production.
Conduct research into the feasibility and design of robotic systems, debug programs, and develop automated control systems for various applications including energy systems.
Design and develop new nanotechnology products and systems, engineer production processes for nanotechnology applications, and supervise the work of suppliers or vendors in device design and testing.
Analyze, fabricate, or test fiber-optic links and design electro-optical systems or components to improve photonics applications.
Design energy-efficient photonics products and apply nanotechnology to enhance performance or reduce environmental impact of energy products.
Conduct research or develop automated systems for environmental applications and assist in designing programs to mitigate environmental issues.
Write reports, prepare presentations, and communicate engineering results or recommendations to ensure clarity and compliance with standards.
Review specifications and recommend engineering changes, evaluate technical requirements for processes or product design, and conduct or supervise tests on materials or products.
Perform managerial functions such as analyzing budgets, writing proposals, and planning and evaluating projects while consulting with engineers or executives.
Work on improving the design and performance of energy systems, including solar or wind technologies, by recommending process changes and conducting feasibility studies.
Develop or review fire exit plans and ensure compliance with fire safety codes by reviewing blueprints and plans for new or remodeled buildings.

Prepare and maintain reports of investigation results, including documenting evidence related to fires and fire code violations, and writing detailed inspection reports with corrective recommendations.

Conduct fire inspections, identify hazards, and recommend corrective actions to ensure properties comply with applicable fire codes and regulations, followed by compliance follow-ups on corrective actions taken.
Teach public education programs on fire safety and prevention, including instructing children about fire dangers and training other personnel in fire investigation techniques and safety procedures.
Testify in court cases and prepare detailed documentation related to fires, suspected arson, and fire safety inspections, including collecting and analyzing evidence from fire scenes.
Supervise and evaluate staff performance, including scheduling work assignments, conducting training, and maintaining safety standards across fire department operations and equipment.

Inspect fire fighting equipment and systems, conducting maintenance and minor repairs while overseeing the replacement or repair of defective firefighting equipment and ensuring proper operational function.
Analyze evidence and incident details to determine causes of fires and document findings using non-destructive testing methods, ensuring compliance with procedural standards.
Coordinate efforts with law enforcement and other agencies during investigations, including subpoenaing witnesses and interviewing building occupants to gather sworn testimony.
Maintain fire prevention programs and issue permits for hazardous material handling and public assemblies, while continuously monitoring and optimizing fire safety protocols and procedures.

Conduct inspections and tests of fire protection systems to verify compliance with safety standards, ensuring installed systems operate correctly and making recommendations for equipment modifications as necessary.
Prepare and document administrative duties including compiling records and maintaining accurate maps of inspection data and fire safety guidelines.

emerging_tasks.task: Use drone technology for aerial surveying and monitoring of drilling sites.
Use drone technology for aerial surveys and inspections of mining sites to enhance safety and efficiency.
Assess systems to identify and quantify risk factors.
Develop plans for integration of drone technology into transportation systems for purposes such as delivery of goods or traffic monitoring.
Research or design methods of processing, forming, and firing materials to develop products, such as ceramic dental fillings, unbreakable dinner plates, and telescope lenses.
Review and select materials for products to meet product design and cost requirements.
Use drone technology for site surveying, inspection, and monitoring of infrastructure projects.
Calibrate, maintain, and repair chemical instrumentation sensing elements and sampling system equipment, using calibration instruments and hand tools.
Document results from radiation and contamination surveys.
Enter data into databases.
Inspect, test, and maintain respiratory protection equipment.
Operate drones to capture aerial footage or photographs of crime scenes for further analysis.
Operate drones to monitor and assess fire conditions, track fire progress, and identify safe access points for firefighters.
Write radiological work permits.
Operate remote sensing equipment on drones to collect data in areas that are difficult to access or require high-resolution imagery.
Analyze meteorological data.
Design electrical interconnections.
Design wind turbine components.
Develop or modify building codes and fire codes.
Estimate energy production by analyzing wind data.
Perform testing on fire protection equipment or building features, such as fire doors or smoke control systems.
Recommend preventive measures to be taken in handling nuclear technology based on data obtained from operations monitoring, systematic analysis, or evaluation of test results.
Review building plans to verify compliance with fire and building codes.
Deploy and monitor drones for aerial surveillance and assessment of fire situations.
Operate drones for remote inspection of large or hard-to-reach structures, such as wind turbines, bridges, or tall buildings.
